title: Luffy Is Forced to Work at the Baratie
description: On pages 2–5, the crew reaches the floating restaurant Baratie, where Luffy’s impulsiveness, Fullbody’s arrogance, and Sanji’s sharp code collide.
scene: The Baratie is loud, violent, and rule-bound: cooks fight like pirates, customers cause trouble, and Sanji’s character begins to emerge through conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What did you say punk? You think you can destroy my restaurant with a cannonball, leave me critically injured,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: And then make it up by only working 1 week without pay...?

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: You're working 1 year and that's final!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No! I waited 10 years just to finally become a pirate!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'll work just 1 week and then you'll forgive me!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Just who gave you the right to decide!? Take this, head chef's kick of justice!!!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I'm the one who'll decide whether I forgive you or not!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Alright, kid. If you're so strapped for time,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Then I'll tell you a faster way to get out of here.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Umm... isn't it obvious I'm not going to choose that option?

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Are you a little dumb in the head or something, old man?

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: saying no to this, saying no to that...

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Don't think you can make it in this world by only saying no, you little brat!

summary: This part introduces the Baratie as more than a restaurant; it is a battlefield of pride, hunger, and harsh principles that will define Sanji’s arc.
